Badger sett present within scrub on the Down at VOT 18m 1323yds near crossing. Sett difficult to access due to vegetation, looks to be one entrance in use at time of discovery <February 2023>, probable Subsidiary or Outlier sett. Lots of badger activity noted in the area including mammal runs and fresh latrines so additional setts in the area are likely. Across the UK, Badgers are protected by UK Law under the `Protection of Badgers Act 1992?. The following activities will all require a license in England and Wales: Using very heavy machinery within 30 metres of any entrance to an active sett; Using lighter machinery <particularly for any digging operation> within 20 metres; Light work such as hand digging or scrub clearance within 10 metres. For guidance seek ecologist advice.

Vegetation management to be undertaken following a Precautionary Working Method Statement for GCN and ToolBox talk to be given to those carrying out the work. The DU"s HSE Advisor <Damon Carberry - 07701 063812>, or Kirsten Daskalova the Hereford DU ecologist 07395391253, can be contacted to provide additional advice and Framework Ecologist details.

Great Crested Newts observed under timbers at crossing. Favourable suitable habitat extends on both the Up and the Down from this location and nearby ponds present. No planned earth works or refugia <sleeper, timbers, slabs, piles of cut/fallen vegetation etc> movement without first consulting an ecologist and possible European Protected Species Licence gained from Natural England. Two hibernacula have been created at the following locations either side of the crossing - WSJ2 173 miles 62.8 ch DOWN utilising newly cut wood and the use of existing wood on the UP at WSJ2 173 miles 62.6 ch. These hibernacula must not be touched/removed; any vegetation work around them will require an ECoW. If any future works are required to renew the crossing, this can only ever be undertaken under a European Protected Species License given known presence of GCN.
